title: Browser BIF/service profile — the 197-row truthful contract, kill-the-lies wiring, and the JS-observable refusal surface
purpose:
The profile pillar governed by the WPORT-5 section of `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:105-116` at `2cfd6cf`. The arc's SCOPE, verbatim: "Define and test a truthful browser contract for registered BIFs versus facilities actually injected into cooperative execution. Supported functions receive working cooperative services. Unsupported functions return stable, explicit errors. Registration must not imply support, and absent services must not degrade into silent no-ops or accidental misbehaviour." (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:110` at `2cfd6cf`). The arc's ACCEPTANCE SHAPE, verbatim: "A profile enumerates every registered browser BIF as supported, supported-with-defined-approximation, or unsupported. Tests execute each supported facility class and assert exact stable errors for unsupported classes. The wall fails on silent success, dropped side effects, environment-dependent behaviour, or a registered function whose required service remains absent and undocumented." (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:114` at `2cfd6cf`). Silent no-ops are the enemy.

SUPERSESSION NOTE, headline and binding (same pattern as WPORT-4): the arc's WPORT-5 boundary evidence is pinned at `d9de35e` and its two-service claim is FALSE since WPORT-3 landed at `a17be58` — `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:112` asserts cooperative bytecode `NativeServices` "currently supplies only the atom table and optional wasm async-NIF facility". At `2cfd6cf`, `WasmScheduler::native_services` supplies THREE services (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:720-731`): `atom_table` Some unconditionally (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:722`); `wasm_async_nif_facility` Some in the browser wrapper — `WasmVm::new` (the wasm_bindgen constructor, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:57-60`; struct at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:44`) builds `HostWasmFacility` and installs it (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:89-97`) — while a bare `WasmScheduler` constructs it `None` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:181`); and `timers` Some, wired to WPORT-3's unified host deadline service (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:724-728`; wheel field and doc at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:140-150`), so `erlang:send_after/3`, `start_timer/3`, and `cancel_timer/1` execute for real. This brief quotes the arc's intent and NEVER its line-pinned boundary claims; every current-state citation below was re-read at the bytes at `2cfd6cf`, and the arc board receives the `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:112` correction line at fold. The remaining 17 wasm-visible `NativeServices` fields are `None` via `..NativeServices::default()` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:729`; the full struct is `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/mod.rs:49-132` — `local_send`, `spawn_facility`, `remote_spawn_facility`, `link_facility`, `group_leader_facility`, `supervision_facility`, `process_info_facility`, `code_management_facility`, `system_info_facility`, `ets_facility`, `io_message_facility`, `replay_driver`, `capability_audit_sink`, `capability_violation_handler`, `bif_registry`, `nif_private_data`, `suspension_registrar`), and a further 13 fields are cfg-compiled out of the wasm closure entirely (`net`/`threads`/`readiness`/`jit` gates visible in the same struct). Feature baseline: beamr-wasm builds beamr with `default-features = false, features = ["cooperative", "json"]` (`crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:18-21`).

The ground (wport5-ground-pack.md, checker-corrected, all citations re-verified at `2cfd6cf` for this brief) shows the arc's bar is currently failed in three distinct ways, in descending severity:

1. SILENT LIES. The cross-process `Send` opcode silently drops and reports success: with no `local_send` facility the opcode skips delivery, sets x0 to the message, and continues — the code comment says "preserving the pre-facility silent-set-x0 behaviour" (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/messaging.rs:110-133`, fall-through comment at `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/messaging.rs:130-131`). Bytecode `Pid ! Msg` between two processes on the cooperative scheduler is dropped and reports success — THE HEADLINE FINDING. The 8-member sink family returns `ok`/`nil` while output vanishes: `write_to_io_sink` is a no-op under not(threads) (`crates/beamr/src/native/context/mod.rs:1484-1485`), swallowing `io:put_chars/1,2` (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs:11-25` via `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs:116-120`), `io:format/3` (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs:27-34`), `logger:warning/2` (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/misc_bifs.rs:14-28`), and the four gleam_stdlib print BIFs (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/gleam_stdlib_ffi2.rs:16-52`). `erlang:display/1` is a bare Rust `println!` outside the sink machinery entirely, discarded by the wasm32 stdio backend while returning `true` (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:210`). And `erlang:statistics/1` plus `erlang:memory/0,1` fabricate all-zero values presented as genuine data via `.unwrap_or_default()` on the missing facility (`crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:142-145`; `crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:255-260`).

2. HOLLOW OBSERVABILITY. Errored pids surface to the browser as completion kind "errored" with `Value::Null` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:597-604`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:643-650`), and the cooperative scheduler exposes only boolean `has_exit_error` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:674-677`) — no cooperative `take_exit_error` exists, though the threaded scheduler has the precedent (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/execution.rs:182`). Refusal CLASS is stable; refusal REASON (badarg vs Undef-with-MFA vs UnsupportedOpcode) is unobservable from JS today. Any "stable explicit errors" ruling is hollow at the surface it is for until this is fixed.

3. UNVERIFIED CLASSIFICATION. 4 of 197 registered MFAs have wasm execution proof — `erlang:self/0`, `send_after/3`, `start_timer/3`, `cancel_timer/1`, via hand-built `CallExt` bytecode whose imports resolve through the real `bif_registry.lookup` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1697-1710`; module builder at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1724-1831`). The remaining 193 registered MFAs — including every WORKS row — are bytes-verified but execution-unverified; the wasm CI gate is two `cargo check` closures plus one Node execution leg pinned to exactly 18 named tests (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:17-20`, `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:34-37`, `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:41-60`, summary `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:68-73` at `2cfd6cf`; WPORT-4 extends this to 26 before this brief builds).

CRITICAL-PATH FRAMING: WPORT-5 sits on the browser-as-peer path (WPORT-4 → WPORT-5 → WPORT-10 → the frame prototype). A browser peer whose `Pid ! Msg` silently drops is not a peer. The wiring half of this brief (R2) is critical-path-essential; the classification half (R1) is the contract that keeps it honest; R3 makes both permanent in the Node gate.

PIN DISCIPLINE, binding: ground evidence is pinned at `2cfd6cf`. Main is already `c6cd26a` — re-verified for this draft: the `2cfd6cf..c6cd26a` diff touches only `crates/beamr/src/telemetry/lifecycle.rs` and `crates/beamr/src/telemetry/lifecycle_tests.rs` (telemetry-only; zero BIF-surface delta). WPORT-4 lands BEFORE the WPORT-5 build and touches the same files (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs`) plus the CI workflow (18 → 26 pinned names), so every lib.rs/wasm.rs/workflow line citation in this brief SHIFTS at the WPORT-4 pin. The build worker MUST re-verify the service-injection truth table and the 197-entry registration total at its actual base commit before writing a line: the expected delta to the registry from WPORT-4 is NONE (WPORT-4 registers no BIFs), but that is a claim to re-check, not to believe — believed state is not citable state.
task:
REGISTERED-SURFACE GROUND (re-verified at `2cfd6cf`): `WasmVm::new` → `register_wasm_safe_bifs` = gate1 + gate2 + stdlib stubs, unfiltered (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1322-1330`). Gate1 registers 21 erlang-core entries (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:23-45`) and chains the sub-families, with net/fs registration blocks compiled out (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:59-80`): code management 6 (`crates/beamr/src/native/code_management_bifs.rs:42-53` plus `code:all_loaded/0` at `crates/beamr/src/native/code_management_bifs.rs:66-67`), dictionary 6 (`crates/beamr/src/native/dictionary_bifs.rs:14-21`), ETF 6, ETS 23, exception 1 (`crates/beamr/src/native/exception_bifs.rs:11`), process_info 4, system_info 7 (`crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:20-28` table). Gate2 registers 17 process-lifecycle entries (`crates/beamr/src/native/process_bifs/mod.rs:21-44`). The stdlib stub table registers 106 (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/registrations.rs:73-658`). Total static registrations: 197, plus two open-ended dynamic seams — `wasm_ffi:js_callback/N` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:152-164`) and host async NIFs via `register_async_nif` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:167-187`). Duplicate-MFA shadowing is impossible: registration rejects duplicates with `NativeRegistrationError::DuplicateMfa` (`crates/beamr/src/native/mod.rs:186-196`; `crates/beamr/src/native/mod.rs:258-268`) — a positive invariant the profile records. Behavior-class tallies carried from the checker-corrected ground pack (§2.2, its DISAGREEMENT resolutions authoritative): 130 WORKS / 51 BADARG / 9 SILENT NO-OP / 3 FABRICATED zeros / 1 PROC-ERROR (`timer:sleep/1`) / 3 documented APPROXIMATIONS under the dominant-class convention (alternate split-BIF convention: 132/49). These tallies are draft INPUT, not repository truth: R1's registry seal re-derives the row set mechanically at the build head.

TEAR RULINGS (Waffles the Terrible, 2026-07-17, torn APPROVED at `ee7a366` — all eight OQs ruled; folded here as decided text; fold base `e58e1ef` is `ee7a366`'s tree rebased byte-identically onto main `53fc8b5`. The tear pass verified five load-bearing citations at the pin own-hands — the `lib.rs:23-25` threads-gating, the not(threads) `write_to_io_sink` no-op, the `display/1` `println!` bypass, the `native_services` seam at `wasm.rs:566-572`, and the gate3 send drop — all exact):

OQ1 ACCEPTED as proposed: keep the facility-absent badarg idiom; no new error atom; the per-MFA conflation documentation lives IN the table rows (defined behavior where the reader looks); ServiceUnavailable push-down is recorded future work, not this brief.

OQ2 ACCEPTED: console default. For a browser runtime the console IS the platform sink — platform-native, not an assumed default — and it kills the silent class by construction. The defined IO line and stream tags are ratified as R2 item 4 specifies.

OQ3 ACCEPTED: flip the fabricated zeros to badarg with the wall. Zeros-as-genuine-data are lies with a decimal point; refusal is the honest surface.

OQ4 ACCEPTED, sink stays IN — with one STOP RIDER binding on the build worker: if un-gating the sink surface cascades beyond the io-module boundary and the sink trait itself (anything pulling thread machinery into the cooperative closure), the worker STOPs and returns rather than improvising the cut. Discovered cascade is the split trigger, not preemptive fear.

OQ5 CLOSED (Tom + Waffles — joint authority complete per the WPORT-4 Ruling 5 precedent): absorption BLESSED. WPORT-5 absorbs the output sink, the `display/1` treatment, and process-error exposure; the WPORT-7-remainder arc line (scheduler/pump failure surface, panic hook, ordered-output guarantees) lands at this fold as the price.

OQ6 ACCEPTED as drafted, one RIDER: the `#[doc(hidden)]` accessor is additive read-only and carries a doc-comment naming the seal test as its only intended consumer — and the cannot-drift reasoning for why the seal must call the real wrapper composition rather than recompose goes in that comment too.

OQ7 ACCEPTED: 33 with the names as listed in R3. Any fold- or build-time delta to that number states the delta and its cause in the same commit that moves it.

OQ8 RULED as pre-signaled: plain spawn/3,4 only; link/monitor-bearing variants stay explicit refusals citing WPORT-4 Ruling 7. Converting a documented latent gap into reachable silent wrong behavior is the one move this campaign exists to forbid.

FIFTEEN PROPOSED RULINGS (Artemis's outline §2 dispositions, elaborated with re-verified ground; originally proposed — the tear has RULED, see TEAR RULINGS above; the P-texts stand as the decided rationale under those rulings):

P1 — REFUSAL VOCABULARY (tear-decisive; also OQ1): KEEP the facility-absent badarg idiom; introduce no new error atom. Distinguishability is met STATICALLY by the R1 table, not dynamically. The dominant idiom `context.<facility>().ok_or_else(badarg)?` produces a catchable `error:badarg` via the native raise mapping (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/native_call.rs:205-224`), semantically overloaded with genuine argument errors; the designed-but-unused fix exists — `ExecError::ServiceUnavailable { service }` (`crates/beamr/src/error.rs:106-116`) is used only for disabled dirty pools (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/execution/core.rs:1604-1621`), never for a BIF-facility refusal. Registry-wide push-down touches the shared native scheduler and every facility gate — wrong brief; RECORDED as future work in the handoff register. The conflation becomes DEFINED behavior once documented per-MFA.

P2 — JS-BOUNDARY REASONS: YES, in scope. Add cooperative `take_exit_error` to `WasmScheduler` (threaded precedent `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/execution.rs:182`; today only boolean `has_exit_error`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:674-677`) and surface the refusal reason through `WasmVm`, so badarg vs `Undef`-with-MFA vs `UnsupportedOpcode` are distinguishable from JS. Small, and it makes every other ruling observable — the ground's own words: rulings are hollow without it. DRAFTER FLAG (carried, not absorbed): this is inside the frozen arc's WPORT-7 scope — see the WPORT-7 COLLISION paragraph below and OQ5.

P3 — SILENT PRINTS (biggest new surface; also OQ2, OQ5): give the cooperative build a real IO-sink seam. `write_to_io_sink` gets a real body on the cooperative path routing to a wrapper-registered host sink on `WasmVm`; DEFAULT = browser console (js-sys is already a dependency, `crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:22`), so output goes somewhere with zero configuration and the 8-member silent class dies. `erlang:display/1` reroutes through the same sink on the cooperative path, with the threaded `println!` path byte-unchanged (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:210` is the only `println!` call site in `native/`). The sink API carries a stream tag (out/err); the gleam `print_error`/`println_error` wrappers — which today intentionally share the stdout sink (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/gleam_stdlib_ffi2.rs:20-24`; `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/gleam_stdlib_ffi2.rs:30-34`) — route err. `io:format/2` and `io:get_line/1` STAY explicit-unsupported: they fail `context.io_message_facility().ok_or_else(badarg)` before any send or suspend (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs:215`); the suspend-forever path (`request_suspend` at `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs:223`) is reachable only on builds that wire an `IoMessageFacility`, and naive wiring is FORBIDDEN by the group-leader self-loop hazard — every wasm-spawned process is its own group leader (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:304`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:346`; native roots `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:201`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:445`), so an io_request would suspend awaiting a reply the caller must produce itself. The format/2-vs-format/3 asymmetry (loud badarg vs silent success for the same user intent) becomes a DEFINED line: sink-based output works, io-protocol IO is unsupported-explicit; a real IO server is a recorded future brief.

P4 — FABRICATED ZEROS (behavior change; also OQ3): reclassify `erlang:statistics/1` and `erlang:memory/0,1` to the dominant explicit-badarg idiom on the missing facility, replacing `.unwrap_or_default()` (`crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:142-145`; `crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:255-260`). Zeros presented as genuine data are worse than refusal. Needs tear blessing plus a wall. `erlang:system_info/1` is already a genuine split and is NOT touched: `wordsize`/`otp_release`/`version`/`system_architecture` answer facility-free (`crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:122-133`) while `schedulers`/`process_count`/`process_limit`/`atom_count`/`atom_limit` badarg via `facility_small_int` (`crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:234-244`).

P5 — CROSS-PROCESS `!` AND SPAWN (critical-path-essential; also OQ8): WIRE `CooperativeLocalSend` into bytecode `native_services` so the headline drop dies — the implementation exists and runs for native slices today (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:166-182`), constructed per slice (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:365-371`) and handed to `NativeContext` with the timer wheel (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:385`), with deferred effects applied after the slice (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:394`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:412-428`). The bytecode side must mirror that shape at the `native_services()`/`run_with_native_services` seam (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:566-572`): per-drain facility construction over a shared effects buffer plus effects application after bytecode slices. DRAFTER FLAG, byte-verified and carried per the ENC-001/WPORT-4 standard (not absorbed, not overridden): the outline's claim that wiring `CooperativeSpawn` makes "the spawn-family badargs become WORKS rows" OVER-CLAIMS at the bytes. `CooperativeSpawn` implements only `spawn_native` (factory-based); every MFA-based method — `spawn`, `spawn_monitor`, `spawn_lambda`, `spawn_lambda_monitor`, `spawn_with_options`, `spawn_lambda_with_options` — returns `Err(SpawnError::UnresolvedMfa)` with the comment "WR-0 spike: only native spawning is exercised cooperatively" (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:81-163`, MFA refusals at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:82-92`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:114-162`). Wiring alone converts the spawn family's facility-absent badarg into a DIFFERENT refusal, not into support. Making bytecode `erlang:spawn/3,4` genuinely work requires implementing MFA spawn on the cooperative path (owned-arg capture, deferred MFA-spawn record, materialization via module lookup — the `spawn_in` shape at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:295-321`). PROPOSED resolution carried to the tear: wire `local_send` + implement MFA spawn for PLAIN `spawn/3,4` only; every link/monitor-bearing variant (`spawn_link`, `spawn_monitor`, `spawn_opt`, and the `link`/`unlink`/`monitor`/`demonitor`/`exit/2` facility family) STAYS explicit-refusal this brief, because bytecode-process exits perform no link propagation — WPORT-4 Ruling 7 recorded that latent gap as unreachable precisely while no link/spawn facility is injected, and wiring link-bearing spawns would make it REACHABLE as silent wrong behavior of exactly the banned class. The linking family belongs to the future bytecode-linking brief WPORT-4 named.

P6 — BIF_REGISTRY INJECTION: WIRE it — a genuine one-liner. The scheduler already holds `bif_registry: Arc<BifRegistryImpl>` as a constructor-injected field (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:121`; ctor `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:161-169`; accessor `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:204-206`) and omits it from `native_services()`, so export-funs and `apply` to any registered BIF fail with `Undef` even though static `CallExt` to the same BIF works (dispatch consumes `services.bif_registry` at `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/closures.rs:154-163`).

P7 — TIMER:SLEEP/1: classify unsupported-with-explicit-process-fatal-error and PIN the untested mapping. Any dirty-registered native returns `InstructionOutcome::DirtyCall` BEFORE the native fn is invoked (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/native_call.rs:96-104`), and the cooperative scheduler converts it to `ExecError::UnsupportedOpcode { name: "dirty native call on wasm" }` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:599-607`); the `std::thread::sleep` body (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/collection_bifs.rs:181`) is unreachable on wasm. The error channel is a scheduler-level process error, not a catchable badarg, and it has ZERO tests today. Reroute-as-yielding-sleep is real design work with WPORT-3 surface implications — recorded future work, not done here.

P8 — CAPABILITY POSTURE: NO restriction in WPORT-5 — the browser keeps `CapabilitySet::all()` (default at `crates/beamr/src/native/capability.rs:117-121`; wasm processes get it at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:303`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:345`; native roots `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:200`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:444`). Fix the two pure-function misregistrations as hygiene NOW: `io:setopts/2` and `io_lib:format/2` carry `Capability::ExternalIo` (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/registrations.rs:418-425`; `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/registrations.rs:426-433`) despite touching no IO (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs:57-63`; `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs:65-71`). Restriction without first fixing the hazards would mass-produce the banned refusal shape: capability denial returns `{error, capability_denied}` as a NORMAL VALUE, not an exception (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/native_call.rs:63-90`, denial return `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/native_call.rs:88-89`, tuple construction `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/native_call.rs:266-276`), and the fallback violation handler is stderr — which goes nowhere on wasm32 (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/native_call.rs:86`). Both hazards are RECORDED verbatim as preconditions in the future capability brief.

P9 — GUARD-POSITION SEMANTICS: accept BEAM guard-swallowing as DEFINED behavior; no code change. Guard-BIF dispatch builds a context carrying ONLY the atom table (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/guards.rs:202-207`) — not even the now-populated timers — and any BIF error in guard position becomes a fail-label jump rather than an exception (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/guards.rs:215-222`), so a missing-facility badarg is silent branch selection. A non-Native guard import fails as `ExecError::InvalidOperand("guard bif native import")` (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/guards.rs:193-195`) — a different shape from the call-path `Undef`. R1's table carries a guard-legal column noting this per guard-legal BIF.

P10 — EXECUTION-PROOF BAR: one representative wall per profile class plus the four unpinned gaps (the DirtyCall→UnsupportedOpcode mapping; the Send-opcode silent drop, whose only spec today is the code comment; call-time `Undef` on the wasm scheduler; `cancel_timer → false` as a missing-service answer on a bare context). The full 193-MFA execution sweep is NOT this brief — the browser+Worker probe pillar proceeds in parallel on the WPORT-3 Ruling 3 terms — authored-not-run, pillar stays OPEN — re-affirmed as WPORT-4 tear Ruling 9. (DRAFTER CORRECTION, carried-not-absorbed: the outline cited "WPORT-3 Ruling 9 precedent"; no such ruling exists — WPORT-3's tear folded exactly three rulings (fold commit 9d0c311) and the parallel-pillar terms are its Ruling 3; "Ruling 9" is WPORT-4's re-affirmation. Corrected at the bytes; outline author concurs.)

P11 — LATENT REGRESSIONS TO PIN: `cancel_timer/1` returns atom `false` when `context.cancel_timer` yields `None` (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:298-304`) — with the facility wired this means "already fired/cancelled", but on a bare context the same `false` is a missing-service answer indistinguishable from it; pin the bare-context shape as a unit test. Pin the sink default so no future BIF can route output silently (a captured-sink wall red-lines any new `write_to_io_sink` caller that is not classified).

P12 — UNREGISTERED SURFACES: `Undef` is the ACCEPTED unsupported shape for the five CLI-only surfaces — observable after P2 lands (take_exit_error carries the MFA). Calls into them resolve to no target → load-time unresolved-import report (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1332-1350`) + call-time `ExecError::Undef`, which has THREE producers the profile documents: `Unresolved` import, `Denied` (load-time capability policy, `crates/beamr/src/module.rs:40-43`), and `Deferred` whose module is still unloaded at call time (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/core.rs:561-573`; `crates/beamr/src/module.rs:44-52`). R1 names all five surfaces (`crates/beamr-cli/src/main.rs:353-361`): gate3 with exactly 59 entries, register_selector_bifs, register_gleam_ffi_bifs, register_meridian_ffi, and otp_stubs, plus the compiled-out net/fs families (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:61-80`). Gate3-for-browser stays out of scope; its two success-shaped traps are recorded as MUST-FIX-BEFORE-REGISTER preconditions: `erlang:send/2` silently drops local non-self sends (`crates/beamr/src/native/gate3_bifs/mod.rs:188-213`, drop documented at `crates/beamr/src/native/gate3_bifs/mod.rs:191-193`) and `is_process_alive/1` returns `false` without a supervision facility (`crates/beamr/src/native/gate3_bifs/mod.rs:617-620`).

P13 — INIT:STOP/1: document caller-only exit as the DEFINED approximation plus a comment at the site. The exit code is validated then discarded (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/misc_bifs.rs:144-152`, discard at `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/misc_bifs.rs:149`) and `request_shutdown` maps to `InstructionOutcome::Exit(ExitReason::Normal)` for the calling process only (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/native_call.rs:199`; `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/native_call.rs:253-255`); no VM-wide shutdown exists on the wasm scheduler. Same record-only pattern as WPORT-4 Ruling 7.

P14 — SPLIT-BIF SUB-ROWS: YES — per-item sub-rows, not one dominant class per MFA, using the checker's carve-out inventory verbatim: `erlang:exit/1` raises facility-free and WORKS (`crates/beamr/src/native/process_bifs/mod.rs:285-293`); `process_flag(trap_exit,_)` badargs on the missing link facility while `process_flag(priority,_)` works process-locally (`crates/beamr/src/native/process_bifs/mod.rs:184-205`); `link/1`/`unlink/1` to SELF return `true` facility-free (`crates/beamr/src/native/process_bifs/mod.rs:124-126`; `crates/beamr/src/native/process_bifs/mod.rs:159-161`); remote pids under not(net): `link/1` → explicit `noproc` (`crates/beamr/src/native/process_bifs/mod.rs:145-146`), `unlink/1` → silent `Ok(true)` (`crates/beamr/src/native/process_bifs/mod.rs:179-180`), `exit/2` → `Ok(true)` while undeliverable (`crates/beamr/src/native/process_bifs/mod.rs:324-327`, wire-spec ruling 7 best-effort) — low reach since Send to a remote pid raises `NoConnection` (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/messaging.rs:29-46`, not(net) arm `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/messaging.rs:44-45`), but all three are classified; `process_info(self(), priority)` answers facility-free (`crates/beamr/src/native/process_info_bifs.rs:214-218`) while `group_leader/2` badargs (`crates/beamr/src/native/process_info_bifs.rs:350`); `system_info/1` splits per P4.

P15 — DYNAMIC SEAMS: classify both. `wasm_ffi:js_callback/N`: unregistered callback name → `Err(UNDEF)` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1163-1165`); marshal failure → badarg (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1155-1161`); Promise rejection → error completion, process exits `ExitReason::Error` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:761-767`). Host async NIFs: unregistered MFA at call time → `Err(UNDEF)` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1045-1047`). Bridge execution proof: CITE WPORT-4's native-completion wall pair (its Ruling 4) as the first bridge execution evidence — do not duplicate it here.

WPORT-7 COLLISION, drafter-raised and carried openly (OQ5): the frozen arc assigns "an intentional browser output sink", "the treatment of `erlang:display/1`", and "expose process execution errors" to WPORT-7 (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:131-140` at `2cfd6cf`, scope verbatim at `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:136`). P2 and P3 pull that core into WPORT-5. The outline does not name WPORT-7. Absorbing frozen arc scope is a supersession that needs its own tear ruling (WPORT-4 Ruling 5 precedent: scope supersession is joint authority) — the tear must either bless the absorption with an arc-board line redefining WPORT-7's remainder (scheduler/pump failure surface, panic hook, ordered-output guarantees) or cut P2/P3 back to WPORT-7. This draft carries the outline's placement as the PROPOSAL and builds R2 assuming it, with the sink seam engineered as the natural split (see boundaries).

DEPENDENCY NOTE: the arc's frozen dependency line is "WPORT-1 and WPORT-2" (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:108` at `2cfd6cf`). This brief declares `depends_on: WPORT-4` — truthful and stronger: WPORT-4 (with its own WPORT-2/WPORT-3 edges) lands first and moves this brief's cited files, so the dependency edge carries the pin-shift caveat from the purpose block. WPORT-1/2 are landed and subsumed.

BINDING LAWS restated where they bind: NO-POLLING verbatim — "NO-POLLING (Tom's ruling): event-loop-driven scheduling — run-to-idle, wake on message; a polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION (F-0d acceptance). Any timer whose job is 'check whether something changed' is a design error." (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:19-23` at `2cfd6cf`) — the R2 sink is PUSH-ONLY: writes flush to the host sink synchronously at the write, no flush timer, no buffer-poll, ever. ZERO new `#[allow]`. No `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic` outside `#[cfg(test)]`. 500-code-line file law (lib.rs entrypoint 400): new surfaces land in new modules, never by growing existing files past the law. The wasm closure stays `cooperative,json` (`crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:18-21`). `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` untouchable (Vesper's claim). No new `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call sites outside tests (WPORT-4's headline prohibition, inherited verbatim — the sink and the wiring consume existing arbiter surface only). Fresh-lint law with Compiling-line capture. Fail-firsts recorded-and-reverted. CI pinned-name + summary-count extension in the SAME COMMIT as the tests. Believed state is not citable state: every current-state claim is file:line at `2cfd6cf` (or the successor pin at dispatch), re-read at the bytes, never a research artifact.

requirements.title: The browser BIF profile table — 197 classified rows, sub-rows, hazards, and a registry seal that cannot drift
requirements.spec:
Author `docs/design/beamr/BROWSER-BIF-PROFILE.md` (placement RATIFIED — OQ6 accepted as drafted): the truthful browser contract enumerating every registered MFA as supported / supported-with-defined-approximation / unsupported, re-derived at the build head, with the checker-corrected ground tallies (130/51/9/3/1/3 at `2cfd6cf`) as the starting inventory to re-verify, never to believe.

The table MUST carry: (1) one row per registered MFA with class, the exact refusal shape for every unsupported row (catchable facility-absent badarg via `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/native_call.rs:205-224`; `Undef` with its three producers; `UnsupportedOpcode` process-fatal for the dirty gate; value-shaped answers listed as VIOLATIONS pending their R2 fix), and the post-R2 target class where R2 changes it — the table records both the found state and the shipped state. (2) Per-item SUB-ROWS for the split BIFs using the P14 carve-out inventory verbatim (process_flag/2, process_info/1,2, system_info/1, link/1, unlink/1, exit/1 vs exit/2, group_leader/0 vs /2, remote-pid rows). (3) Approximation text for every defined-approximation row, including at minimum: `erlang:fun_info/2` fabricates `module`/`name`/`type` as the item name itself with `env` always `[]` (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/misc_bifs.rs:165-183`, fabrication at `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/misc_bifs.rs:178`) — wrong data as success, the worst approximation in the table; `io_lib:format/2` returns a binary where OTP returns a charlist, with only a directive subset supported and unknown directives badarg-ing explicitly (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs:65-71`); `lists:member/2` uses numeric `==` where OTP uses `=:=` (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/lists_bifs.rs:80-84`); `lists:seq/2` returns `[]` for `From > To` where OTP raises (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/lists_bifs.rs:233-235`); `io:setopts/2` success-returning no-op (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/io_bifs.rs:57-63`, carried WORKS-as-defined-stub unless the tear reclassifies); `sys:debug_options/1` → `[]` (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/misc_bifs.rs:154-163`); `init:stop/1` caller-only exit per P13; the gleam stderr/stdout conflation pre-R2 (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/gleam_stdlib_ffi2.rs:20-24`, `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/gleam_stdlib_ffi2.rs:30-34`); `rand:uniform/0` non-seedable host entropy via getrandom `wasm_js` (`crates/beamr/Cargo.toml:95-96`); plus the string/base64/math/binary shape rows from the ground pack §2.3, each re-verified at the bytes before the row is written. (4) A guard-legal column per P9 noting that a missing-facility badarg in guard position is silent fail-branch selection (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/guards.rs:202-222`) and that guard contexts receive only the atom table. (5) The unregistered-surface context set per P12: the five CLI-only surfaces (`crates/beamr-cli/src/main.rs:353-361`), gate3's 59 entries, the accepted `Undef` posture, the three `Undef` producers, and the gate3 MUST-FIX-BEFORE-REGISTER preconditions (`crates/beamr/src/native/gate3_bifs/mod.rs:188-213`; `crates/beamr/src/native/gate3_bifs/mod.rs:617-620`). (6) The dynamic-seam rows per P15. (7) The recorded hazards: the capability value-shaped denial + stderr handler + the two ExternalIo misregistrations (P8 cites); the group-leader self-loop deadlock hazard (P3 cites); duplicate-MFA impossibility as a positive invariant (`crates/beamr/src/native/mod.rs:258-268`).

THE SEAL — the table cannot drift silently: add an additive read-only enumeration accessor to `BifRegistryImpl` (none exists at `2cfd6cf`: the impl exposes only `new`/`register*`/`lookup`/`coverage`, `crates/beamr/src/native/mod.rs:287-360`) returning the registered `(module, function, arity, capability)` set, and a plain native `#[test]` in `crates/beamr-wasm/tests/profile_seal.rs` (native-test precedent in that crate: `tests/generated_bootstrap.rs` runs as a plain test — note it uses only `std::fs` + `env!` and links no lib internals; `tests/` is a separate crate that reaches only the lib's PUBLIC API) that builds a fresh registry through the real wrapper composition. `register_wasm_safe_bifs` is PRIVATE at `2cfd6cf` (`fn register_wasm_safe_bifs(`, no `pub`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1322-1330`), so this requirement adds a minimal declared visibility surface in `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs` — a `#[doc(hidden)] pub fn build_wasm_safe_registry()` helper (or a `#[doc(hidden)] pub` re-export of `register_wasm_safe_bifs`) that wraps the private composition unchanged — which the seal test calls. Recomposing the registry inside the test from beamr's pub `register_gate1_bifs`/`register_gate2_bifs`/`register_stdlib_stubs` is FORBIDDEN for the seal: it duplicates the wrapper's composition and forfeits exactly the cannot-drift property (a future filter or fourth chain added inside `register_wasm_safe_bifs` would go uncaught). The test parses the profile document's machine-readable row keys, and asserts exact set equality both ways — a registered-but-unlisted MFA and a listed-but-unregistered row BOTH fail. The document format therefore carries one machine-parseable row key per MFA (fenced table with `module:function/arity` keys — mechanism RATIFIED, OQ6). The seal test also asserts the row count so the 197 total (± the R2 delta, expected zero new registrations) is pinned mechanically. OQ6 RIDER (tear-ruled): the `#[doc(hidden)]` visibility surface and the enumeration accessor each carry a doc-comment naming the seal test as their only intended consumer, including the cannot-drift reasoning (recomposing the registry in-test forfeits exactly the property the seal exists for).
acceptance: The profile document exists, carries one classified row per registered MFA including every sub-row named in P14, and states the exact refusal shape (badarg / Undef / UnsupportedOpcode / documented approximation) for every non-WORKS row; no row's class is 'registered' — registration nowhere implies support

Every defined-approximation row carries its approximation text with a file:line citation re-verified at the build head; `erlang:fun_info/2`'s fabrication, the io_lib:format shape deviation, and the lists rows are present verbatim
The guard-legal column exists and names silent fail-branch selection for every guard-legal facility-touching BIF

The unregistered-surface section names all five CLI-only surfaces plus the compiled-out net/fs families, documents the three Undef producers, and carries the two gate3 MUST-FIX-BEFORE-REGISTER preconditions

The seal test fails when a row is deleted from the document (fail-first: remove one row, observe red, revert) and when a synthetic MFA is registered in the test registry without a row (proven by construction in the test itself); the enumeration accessor is read-only and additive — zero behavior change to registration or lookup — and both the accessor and the `#[doc(hidden)]` helper carry the OQ6-ruled doc-comment naming the seal test as sole intended consumer with the cannot-drift reasoning

The hazards section records the capability value-shaped denial, the stderr violation-handler discard on wasm32, the ExternalIo misregistrations (pre-R2), the group-leader self-loop, and duplicate-MFA impossibility, each with citations

requirements.title: Kill the lies — local send, plain MFA spawn, bif_registry, the cooperative IO sink, honest statistics, capability hygiene, and JS-observable refusal reasons
requirements.spec:
Seven wiring items plus one comment-only edit, each cited to its ground, each leaving the threaded/native scheduler byte-identical in behavior (the threaded service construction, threaded `write_to_io_sink` body at `crates/beamr/src/native/context/mod.rs:1479-1482`, and threaded `display/1` `println!` path are NOT touched semantically).

(1) LOCAL SEND (the headline): inject a `CooperativeLocalSend`-shaped `local_send` facility into bytecode `NativeServices` at the `native_services()`/`run_with_native_services` seam (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:566-572`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:720-731`), mirroring the native-slice shape — shared `DeferredEffects` buffer, facility constructed per drain (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:166-182`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:365-371`), effects applied after the slice exactly as `apply_deferred_effects` does for native slices (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:394`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:412-428`), including the ready-wake so a delivered message makes the target runnable within the same drain's discipline. The Send opcode's silent fall-through (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/messaging.rs:110-133`) then takes the facility path; the fall-through branch itself is NOT deleted (bare `run()` with no scheduler keeps its documented behavior) — the profile row for `!` on the cooperative scheduler flips to WORKS.

(2) PLAIN MFA SPAWN (per P5's flagged resolution; OQ8 RULED: plain spawn/3,4 only): implement MFA spawn on the cooperative bytecode path for `erlang:spawn/3` and `spawn/4` only — owned-argument capture at the facility, a deferred MFA-spawn record, and post-slice materialization through `module_registry.lookup_mfa` following the `spawn_in` construction shape (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:295-321`: `CapabilitySet::all()`, self group leader, Normal priority, ready push with edge accounting). Every link/monitor-bearing spawn variant and the link/monitor/exit-2 facility family REMAIN explicit refusals, with their profile rows citing WPORT-4 Ruling 7's latent no-link-propagation gap as the reason (bytecode exits perform no link propagation; wiring link-bearing spawns would make that gap reachable as silent wrong behavior). OQ8 ruled the scope exactly as specified — plain spawn/3,4 is IN; the conditional-cut branch is retired.

(3) BIF_REGISTRY: add `bif_registry: Some(Arc::clone(&self.bif_registry))` to `native_services()` — the field exists on the scheduler (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:121`) and the consumer exists in closure dispatch (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/closures.rs:154-163`); export-fun/`apply`-to-BIF `Undef` dies.

(4) COOPERATIVE IO SINK (per P3; OQ2 RULED: console default ratified; OQ4 RULED: sink stays in, STOP rider below): give the cooperative build a real sink seam. Proposed mechanism (drafter's; the worker may refine at the same surface): un-gate an optional sink slot for the cooperative path so `ProcessContext::write_to_io_sink` writes to an injected sink when present instead of the unconditional not(threads) no-op (`crates/beamr/src/native/context/mod.rs:1484-1485`), threaded body untouched. SEAM SIZE, stated honestly: at `2cfd6cf` the ENTIRE `io` module — and with it the `IoSink` trait (`crates/beamr/src/io/mod.rs:227-231`) — is threads-gated out of the cooperative closure at `crates/beamr/src/lib.rs:23-25`, and the `NativeServices` sink slot is likewise cfg(threads)-gated (`crates/beamr/src/interpreter/mod.rs:84-85`); this item therefore necessarily edits beamr-side surface, not just the wrapper: make the trait reachable from the cooperative closure (un-gate the trait definition or relocate it) and un-gate the sink slot, with every existing threaded `IoSink` impl compiling unchanged. Extend the sink vocabulary with a stream tag (out/err) via an ADDITIVE default method so existing threaded `IoSink` impls compile unchanged; `WasmScheduler` carries the injected sink into `native_services()`; `WasmVm` exposes `register_io_sink(callback)` (JS callback receiving `(stream, bytes-or-string)`) and installs a js-sys console default at construction — console.log for out, console.error for err — so zero-configuration output goes somewhere and the silent class dies. All sink machinery lives in a new `crates/beamr-wasm/src/io_sink.rs` module wired through lib.rs (500-line law). Reroute `erlang:display/1` through `write_to_io_sink` on the cooperative path only, threaded `println!` byte-identical (`crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:210`). Route gleam `print_error`/`println_error` with the err tag (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/gleam_stdlib_ffi2.rs:20-24`, `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/gleam_stdlib_ffi2.rs:30-34`). The sink is PUSH-ONLY: synchronous write-through at the BIF, no flush timer, no recurring callback — NO-POLLING binds. Every scheduler borrow drops before the host sink callback is invoked (lib.rs borrow discipline, binding on the sink exactly as the WPORT-3 pen note states it for the deadline service). `io:format/2` and `io:get_line/1` stay explicit-unsupported (P3): no `IoMessageFacility` is wired on any branch — the group-leader self-loop (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:304`) makes naive wiring a guaranteed self-deadlock; their profile rows document this. OQ4 STOP RIDER (binding): if un-gating the sink surface cascades beyond the io-module boundary and the sink trait itself — anything pulling thread machinery into the cooperative closure — the worker STOPs and returns rather than improvising the cut; discovered cascade is the split trigger, not preemptive fear.

(5) HONEST STATISTICS (per P4; OQ3 RULED: flip blessed): `erlang:statistics/1` and `erlang:memory/0,1` refuse with the dominant facility-absent badarg instead of `.unwrap_or_default()` zeros (`crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:142-145`; `crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:255-260`); the facility-free `system_info/1` items are untouched (`crates/beamr/src/native/system_info_bifs.rs:122-133`).

(6) CAPABILITY HYGIENE (per P8): reclassify `io:setopts/2` and `io_lib:format/2` from `Capability::ExternalIo` to the correct pure/process-local capability (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/registrations.rs:418-433`) — inert today under `CapabilitySet::all()`, load-bearing for the future capability brief; no other capability rows change.

(7) REFUSAL-REASON SURFACING (per P2; OQ5 CLOSED: absorption blessed, Tom + Waffles joint authority): add cooperative `take_exit_error(pid)` to `WasmScheduler` beside `has_exit_error` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:674-677`; threaded precedent `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/execution.rs:182`) and surface it through `WasmVm` so an errored completion can carry a structured reason — at minimum the `ExecError` variant name plus the MFA for `Undef` — instead of `Value::Null` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:597-604`; `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:643-650`). Additive surface only: existing completion shapes stay valid; the reason rides alongside, never replaces, the "errored" kind.

(8) INIT:STOP SITE COMMENT (per P13, comment-only — the WPORT-4 seam-comment pattern): a comment at the `init:stop/1` stub (`crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/misc_bifs.rs:144-152`, exit-code discard at `crates/beamr/src/native/stdlib_stubs/misc_bifs.rs:149`) stating the DEFINED approximation: `request_shutdown` exits the CALLING PROCESS only (`InstructionOutcome::Exit(ExitReason::Normal)`, `crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/native_call.rs:253-255`); no VM-wide shutdown exists on the wasm scheduler; the validated exit code is discarded. The R1 profile row cites this comment.

PROHIBITIONS inherited verbatim: zero new `#[allow]`; no `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic` outside `#[cfg(test)]`; no WPORT-2/3/4 surface changes; the wasm closure stays `cooperative,json`; `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` untouchable; no new `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call sites outside tests — message delivery and spawn materialization ride the existing drain/edge seams (`note_ready_push` at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:667-671` is the existing accounting, not a new call site).
acceptance: SEND: real bytecode `Pid ! Msg` between two cooperative bytecode processes delivers — the receiver observes the payload, the sender's x0 carries the message, and nothing is dropped; the pre-facility fall-through remains only for scheduler-less bare `run()`

SPAWN (OQ8 ruled: kept — plain spawn/3,4 only): bytecode `erlang:spawn/3` returns a fresh pid whose process runs the named exported function under the cooperative scheduler; `spawn_link`/`spawn_monitor`/`spawn_opt` and `link`/`unlink`/`monitor`/`demonitor`/`exit/2` remain explicit refusals with profile rows citing the WPORT-4 Ruling 7 latent gap
APPLY/EXPORT-FUN: `apply` and export-fun dispatch to a registered BIF succeed where static `CallExt` already did; the `Undef` on that path is gone

SINK: `io:put_chars/1,2`, `io:format/3`, `logger:warning/2`, all four gleam print BIFs, and `erlang:display/1` land in the registered host sink with correct stream tags; with zero configuration they land on the console (console default — OQ2 ratified); threaded-build behavior is byte-identical (grep-provable: the threads `write_to_io_sink` body and the threaded display path unchanged); no flush timer or recurring callback exists on any sink branch

STATISTICS (OQ3 ruled: flip blessed): `erlang:statistics/1` and `erlang:memory/0,1` raise catchable badarg on the cooperative scheduler instead of returning zeros; facility-backed builds are unchanged
CAPABILITY: `io:setopts/2` and `io_lib:format/2` no longer carry ExternalIo; no other registration row changes; zero behavior delta under CapabilitySet::all()

REASONS: an errored pid's completion exposes a structured reason from JS distinguishing facility-absent badarg, `Undef` (with MFA), and `UnsupportedOpcode("dirty native call on wasm")`; existing completion consumers keep working unchanged
INIT:STOP COMMENT: the P13 defined-approximation comment is present at the misc_bifs.rs site (comment-only edit; no behavior change) and the R1 profile row cites it

The final diff adds no `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call site outside tests, zero `#[allow]`, zero non-test `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`; `io:format/2`/`io:get_line/1` still refuse with badarg and no `IoMessageFacility` is constructed anywhere in the wasm closure

requirements.title: Profile walls and the exact-name/exact-count CI extension — the four unpinned gaps sealed, fail-firsts recorded-and-reverted
requirements.spec:
PROPOSED Node-leg wall set (in-crate wasm-bindgen tests on the pinned Node leg; names RATIFIED as listed — OQ7; any fold- or build-time delta to a name or the count states the delta and its cause in the same commit that moves it):

(1) `cross_process_bytecode_send_delivers_and_coalesces_a_burst` — the headline drop, dead: from true idle, one bytecode-to-bytecode send wakes the receiver through the arbiter; a pre-turn burst coalesces; every payload is durable (WPORT-4 wall discipline: counted gauges, no manual pump). (2) `bytecode_spawn_runs_child_under_the_cooperative_scheduler` — plain `spawn/3` end-to-end (OQ8 ruled: kept — plain spawn/3). (3) `apply_and_export_fun_dispatch_registered_bifs_without_undef` — bif_registry wired. (4) `print_family_lands_in_registered_sink_with_stream_tags` — put_chars/format-3/logger/gleam print + display through a registered capturing JS sink, err/out tags asserted, gleam print_error on err. (5) `default_sink_routes_output_to_console_with_zero_configuration` — the console default (RETAINED as its own wall per the OQ7 names-as-listed ruling; the captured-sink wall remains the load-bearing one). (6) `take_exit_error_distinguishes_refusal_reasons_from_js` — one process erroring badarg, one `Undef` (call into an unregistered MFA — pinning call-time `Undef` on the wasm scheduler, unpinned gap), one `timer:sleep/1` (pinning the DirtyCall→UnsupportedOpcode mapping end-to-end from JS, unpinned gap; scheduler-level mapping at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:599-607`). (7) `statistics_and_memory_refuse_explicitly_instead_of_fabricating_zeros` — the P4 flip, asserted as catchable badarg from bytecode (OQ3 ruled: kept).

CORE-SIDE pins (native `#[test]` in `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_tests.rs`, NOT in the Node count): the `cancel_timer → false` bare-context missing-service shape (P11; `crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs:298-304`); a scheduler-level DirtyCall→UnsupportedOpcode unit pin beside the existing bytecode-timer test (`cooperative_bytecode_timer_bifs_round_trip_through_native_services`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_tests.rs:284-526` at `2cfd6cf`); and a send-drop tombstone: a test named for the retired behavior proving delivery now occurs (the old silent drop's only spec was the messaging.rs comment — unpinned gap, sealed here).

SPLITTABILITY — RULED (OQ4 accepted, OQ5 closed): the IO-sink seam stays IN; the WPORT-7 absorption is blessed by joint authority (Tom + Waffles) and the WPORT-7-remainder arc line landed at fold. Expected registry delta from WPORT-4: none — re-checked, not believed.

Run the house bar on the final head: `cargo fmt --check`; fresh-lint per the amended gate law (`cargo clean -p <crate>` before `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, Compiling/Checking line captured beside the exit code; cached greens stamped "checksum-verified cache hit", never "fresh re-lint"); `cargo test --workspace --all-features --lib`; `cargo test --workspace --all-features --test '*'` (includes the R1 profile seal); `npm test --prefix examples/edge-worker`. Run both locked wasm checks: `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p beamr --no-default-features --features cooperative,json --locked` and `cargo check --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked`. Then the pinned Node leg: `CARGO_TARGET_WASM32_UNKNOWN_UNKNOWN_RUNNER=wasm-bindgen-test-runner cargo test --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked -- --nocapture`; require the UPDATED workflow assertion to find every exact name and the exact final summary count (ruled 33 — OQ7), with missing names, stale names, wrong counts, and `no tests to run!` still failing — verified against the actual run output, not the yml alone. Run every new pin in isolation ten consecutive times. Perform, record, and REVERT the four R3 fail-first mutations plus the R1 seal fail-first. Grep-verify the prohibitions: the final diff adds no `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call site outside tests, zero `#[allow]`, zero non-test `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`, no `IoMessageFacility` construction in the wasm closure, and no timer/interval call in the sink module.
